title: Firmprint
problem: Zarna's value proposition is "AI associates tuned to your firm's evaluation methods, decision making patterns, and institutional knowledge" (zarnaai.com). Their go to market is forward deployed engineering — embedding with a fund for weeks to elicit the firm's screening rubric, IC template, and CRM taxonomy, then encoding that into the four associates. That elicitation is the bottleneck.
direction: An open source tool that ingests a fund's historical memo and deal-review corpus and emits a machine readable "firm profile" — screening criteria, IC memo style guide, decision bias matrix — that drops directly into Zarna's Analyst and Memo agents as a system prompt + few shot bundle.
why: Judgment about which problem: Zarna's bottleneck is onboarding time, not output quality. Every other pitch on Vivan's desk will be "another agent." This one compounds Zarna's existing four agents.

archetypes.name: zarna evidence replay
archetypes.trigger: zarna signal changes while value context is stale
archetypes.expected: block release until cited evidence is regenerated

archetypes.name: value boundary probe
archetypes.trigger: value handoff crosses a policy or trust boundary
archetypes.expected: route to reviewer with evidence packet

archetypes.name: proposition regression harness
archetypes.trigger: proposition behavior regresses against the last accepted fixture
archetypes.expected: open a regression issue with trace and benchmark delta

archetypes.name: associates operator packet
archetypes.trigger: associates output needs a human-readable audit packet
archetypes.expected: accept only if decision claims cite fixture evidence
